preview_text:
The model backs Croatia heavily, assigning them a 79% chance of victory compared to Ghana's 3%. The gap reflects a substantial asymmetry in the forecast, with the model seeing this as a relatively one-sided contest on a neutral pitch.

The draw probability of 19% sits between the win probabilities, suggesting the model expects Croatia to control play but Ghana to have enough resilience to avoid defeat outright. The win spread is wide—76 percentage points separate the favorites from the underdog—indicating the model views the sides as far from evenly matched.

The market and Polymarket consensus diverge meaningfully from the model. Both assign Ghana roughly 16% to win, compared to the model's 3%—a gap of 13 percentage points. The sportsbooks rate Croatia at 58%, roughly 20pp lower than the model's 79%. The draw estimates align more closely across sources, clustering near 25%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which forecast is more accurate; the discrepancy warrants attention from users evaluating the full range of available information.
